title: Packed BM25 numericId slots never recycled — monotonic packedDocIds growth under update churn erodes the bounded-RAM claim

findingDetails.evidence: getPackedNumericId (848-850) only ever does packedDocIds.push() / length++; removePackedDocument (959) sets packedDocIds[numericId]=undefined but no free-list or compaction exists (grep for reuse/recycle/compact returns nothing). Every live update (syncChangedRows:743 -> addDocumentFields:537-539 -> removeDocument + re-add) abandons the old slot and pushes a new one. Only clear()/rebuildFromDatabase (668) resets the array.
findingDetails.why: packedDocIds grows monotonically with cumulative write volume between rebuilds rather than tracking live document count. The legacy in-memory engine keyed off a string Map with no such growth, so the packed engine (new this epic) regresses steady-state memory: the '135MB bounded' RAM claim holds only at fresh warmup, not under sustained re-index/update churn.
findingDetails.recommendation: Recycle freed numericIds via a free-list (push undefined slots onto a stack in removePackedDocument; pop in getPackedNumericId before extending), or document that the RAM bound is warmup-time only and rely on periodic rebuildFromDatabase/daemon re-election to compact. A free-list keeps packedDocIds bounded by live doc count.
